```python
class Solution:
    def divisorGame(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n % 2 == 0

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution relies on the mathematical property that Alice wins if and only if n is even. This is because if n is even, Alice can always make a move that leaves Bob with an odd number, forcing Bob to make a move that leaves Alice with an even number again, until n becomes 1. The function simply checks if n is even by verifying if the remainder when divided by 2 is 0.
```
code:
def divisorGame(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n % 2 == 0
